name: Corptax Corporate Tax Software API
what_it_does: Corptax enterprise corporate tax software REST API for corporate income tax compliance, ASC 740 tax provision, and tax workflow management. Enables AI agents to manage federal consolidated return preparation and multi-entity tax data for corporate tax compliance automation, handle state and local income tax apportionment and compliance for multi-jurisdiction tax management, access ASC 740 tax provision and effective tax rate calculation for financial reporting automation, retrieve deferred tax asset/liability scheduling and management for tax balance sheet automation, manage transfer pricing documentation and intercompany transaction analysis for international tax compliance, handle GILTI, FDII, BEAT, and international tax compliance for multinational corporations, access tax data import and GL integration for ERP-to-tax workflow automation, retrieve NOL tracking and carryback/carryforward management for tax attribute management, manage e-file transmission and tax authority submission for electronic filing automation, and integrate Corptax with SAP, Oracle, and enterprise ERP systems.
use_cases: Preparing corporate returns from tax compliance agents
Calculating tax provision from ASC 740 agents
Managing state apportionment from multi-state agents
Tracking NOLs from tax attribute management agents
not_for: Individual or partnership tax (use professional tax software)
Small business tax without C-corp complexity
Tax research and law (use Bloomberg BNA or Checkpoint)
best_when: A large corporate tax department or tax accounting firm wanting AI agents to automate federal consolidated return preparation, ASC 740 tax provision, state apportionment, international tax compliance, and tax data management for enterprise corporate tax.
avoid_when: TAX RETURN ACCURACY AND CPA RESPONSIBILITY: Automated federal and state return data population must be reviewed and approved by CPA or tax professional before filing; automated filing without professional review creates practitioner liability under Treasury Circular 230. ASC 740 EFFECTIVE TAX RATE ACCURACY: Automated tax provision calculation must correctly classify permanent versus temporary differences and apply enacted tax rates; automated provision errors create SEC financial statement misstatement risk. TRANSFER PRICING CONTEMPORANEOUS DOCUMENTATION: Automated transfer pricing documentation must be contemporaneous (prepared at time of transaction) to qualify for penalty protection; automated documentation created after IRS audit notice does not qualify for IRC 6662 penalty protection. GILTI high-tax exception complexity — automated GILTI high-tax exclusion election requires CFE-by-CFE analysis; automated GILTI without jurisdiction-specific HTE election creates suboptimal US tax position.

auth.notes: Corptax uses OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Dallas, Texas HQ. Founded 1996. Subsidiary of Wolters Kluwer (AEX: WKL, acquired 2019). Enterprise corporate tax software. 1,200+ large corporation tax departments. Federal consolidated return, state, and international tax. ASC 740 tax provision specialist. Competes with Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE and Bloomberg BNA for enterprise corporate tax.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription; compliance, provision, and workflow modules
pricing.notes: Dallas TX. Wolters Kluwer subsidiary (acquired 2019). Founded 1996. 1,200+ large corp customers. Federal, state, international tax. ASC 740 provision specialist. Enterprise licensing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; tax calculation and consolidation operations may have extended processing time; documented per enterprise agreement

known_agent_gotchas: ASC 740 TEMPORARY DIFFERENCE CLASSIFICATION: Automated tax provision population must correctly classify each book-tax difference as permanent (no deferred tax) or temporary (creates deferred tax); automated misclassification of temporary as permanent creates effective tax rate error and SEC financial reporting misstatement

NO WEBHOOKS — CONSOLIDATION AND E-FILE STATUS REQUIRE POLLING: Corptax lacks webhook support; federal consolidated return compilation and e-file acknowledgment require polling; implement scheduled polling aligned with tax compliance deadline calendar

GILTI high-tax exception entity-by-entity election — automated GILTI calculation must evaluate high-tax exclusion on a CFC-by-CFC basis; automated aggregate GILTI calculation without HTE analysis creates suboptimal US tax liability and potential regulatory scrutiny

Transfer pricing documentation contemporaneity — automated transfer pricing report generation must be initiated before end of tax year or filing deadline; automated documentation prepared post-audit does not qualify for IRC 6662(e) penalty protection

State apportionment method variance — automated multi-state apportionment must apply each state's specific method (single-sales factor, three-factor, Joyce/Finnegan group apportionment); automated uniform apportionment without state-specific method creates state audit and deficiency assessment

Deferred tax asset realizability assessment — automated DTA balance requires annual realizability assessment under ASC 740-10-30; automated DTA recording without realizability analysis creates overstated deferred tax asset and potential valuation allowance requirement
agent_readiness.security_notes: Corporate tax software. SOC2, IRS efile certified. OAuth2. US. Corporate tax return and provision data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate tax. SOC2, IRS efile. OAuth2. US. Tax return and provision data.
